objective: Final corrective attempt 3/3 for the B4-C fail-closed CLI. Preserve the attempt-2 production behavior and passing regressions, but replace the impossible subprocess-local mocking pattern with an in-process harness for deterministic fault injection. Claude Code OAuth Actions only; no paid API fallback.

material_failures: default argparse emitted stderr for a missing value
direct-script execution lacked repository-root sys.path bootstrap
the repeated-option regression was a pass placeholder and production accepted the last value

working_corrections: direct script from repository root and a different cwd returned exact success with empty stderr
missing option values returned exact cli_argument_invalid JSON with exit 2 and empty stderr
space, equals and mixed-form repeated options were rejected by real production execution
all ordinary success, override, file-content and validation-composition subprocess tests passed
attempt_2.material_failure: All six failures were tests that patched Path.read_bytes, builtins.__import__, validate_registry or validate_execution_plan in the unittest process and then called a helper implemented exclusively with subprocess.run. Those patches cannot cross an exec boundary, so unmodified production ran and returned success. The failure is the test harness, not evidence of a production defect.

attempt_lineage.attempt_3_policy: Preserve the working attempt-2 production algorithm and all passing subprocess regressions. Correct only the six impossible fault-injection tests by adding a genuine in-process harness that imports the exact production file and calls main(argv) while capturing stdout and stderr. No blind redesign.

production_contract.public_entrypoint: main(argv=None) returns an integer process exit code
production_contract.repo_root_bootstrap: insert Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] into sys.path before importing tools modules

invalid: a missing or empty option value
any repeated named option, including mixed --name PATH and --name=PATH forms

validation_order: load registry as strict UTF-8 JSON
load schema as strict UTF-8 JSON
only after registry success load plan as strict UTF-8 text

output.failure: one compact JSON object with exactly errors and valid, non-empty sorted unique errors, valid false and one trailing newline

production_contract.validator_results: preserve merged validator codes after sorted unique normalization, including jsonschema_dependency_missing, schema_definition_invalid and schema_validation_failed

two_harnesses_required.subprocess_harness: Use sys.executable and the absolute production script path for real process-boundary regressions: defaults from repository root and another cwd; complete argument matrix; overrides; normal file and validator composition cases. Assert exact exit, stdout and empty stderr.
two_harnesses_required.in_process_harness: Load the exact production file with importlib.util.spec_from_file_location under a unique non-conflicting module name, execute the module, call its main(argv) directly, and capture stdout and stderr with contextlib.redirect_stdout and redirect_stderr. Use this harness only for deterministic patched fault injection. Restore sys.path/module state and mocks after each test.

fault_injection_must_be_real: Patch pathlib.Path.read_bytes in the same process in which production main executes; inject PermissionError for registry and plan and OSError for schema; assert the exact unreadable code, exit 1, one JSON document and empty stderr.
Patch builtins.__import__ in the same process in which production validate_registry lazily imports jsonschema; assert jsonschema_dependency_missing, exit 1 and empty stderr.
Patch tools.canonical_repository_registry.validate_registry before production main imports it; raise RuntimeError; assert cli_internal_error, exit 1 and empty stderr.
Patch tools.canonical_repository_plan.validate_execution_plan before production main imports it; raise RuntimeError; assert cli_internal_error, exit 1 and empty stderr.
forbidden_test_patterns: expecting a unittest.mock.patch in the parent test process to affect a subprocess created with subprocess.run
mandatory_tests.argument_matrix: include positional, unknown, abbreviated, missing value, empty equals value, and repeated space/equals/mixed forms; execute real production code and assert exact JSON plus empty stderr
mandatory_tests.override_matrix: each named override independently and all together succeed with canonical temporary copies
mandatory_tests.composition: malformed plan, invalid schema definition, schema-invalid registry and lazy-jsonschema absence reach their exact codes
mandatory_tests.output_for_every_case: exactly one parseable compact JSON document, exactly one trailing newline and empty stderr
